source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure encryption in transit is enabled for Lambda environment variables

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure encryption in transit is enabled for Lambda environment variables
input_row.description: As you can set your own environmental variables for Lambda it is important to also encrypt them for in transit protection.
input_row.rationale: Lambda environment variables should be encrypted in transit for client-side protection as they can store sensitive information.
input_row.audit: From the Console 1. Login to the AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/lambda/. 2. In the left column, under AWS Lambda, click Functions. 3. Under Function name click on the name of the function that you want to review 4. Click the Configuration tab 5. In the left column, click Environment variables. 6. In the Environment variables section, click Edit 7. On the Edit environment variables page, review the Values. If they are a long value that resembles this: AQICAHhxbKJYcFAU16CbU4IVpzi5CwK Encryption is in place for that Key. If the value is in plain text refer to the remediation below. 8. Repeat steps 2 – 7 for each Lambda function available within the current AWS region. 9. Repeat this Audit for all the other AWS regions. From the Command line 1. Run 'aws lambda list-functions' aws lambda list-functions --output table --query "Functions[*].FunctionName" This command will provide a table titled ListFunctions  2. Run aws lambda get-function aws lambda get-function --function-name "name_of_function" --query "Configuration.Environment" This will provide an output of the environment variables created for that function. 3. Review the Values in the table. If they contain a long value that resembles this: AQICAHhxbKJYcFAU16CbU4IVpzi5CwK. Encryption is in place for that Key. If the value is in plain text refer to the remediation below. 4. Repeat steps 1 – 3 for each Lambda function listed within the current region. 5. Repeat this Audit for all the other AWS regions.
input_row.remediation: From the Console 1. Login to the AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/lambda/. 2. In the left column, under AWS Lambda, click Functions. 3. Under Function name click on the name of the function that you want to review 4. Click the Configuration tab 5. In the left column, click Environment variables. 6. In the Environment variables section, click Edit 7. Click the check box for Enable helpers for encryption in transit 8. Click the Encrypt option for all the variable that need to be encrypted. 9. Repeat steps 2 – 8 for each Lambda function identified in the Audit within the current AWS region. 10. Repeat this remediation for all the other AWS regions.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be verified using the AWS APIs and CLI commands as mentioned in the audit section, which makes it suitable for automation. It describes using AWS CLI commands such as `aws lambda list-functions` and `aws lambda get-function` to retrieve and verify the configuration of environment variables for Lambda functions, checking whether they follow the encryption pattern.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_lambda_function_environment_variables_encryption_in_transit_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use `boto3` to interact with the AWS Lambda service.
- List all Lambda functions using `boto3.client('lambda').list_functions()` to retrieve function names.
- For each function, use `boto3.client('lambda').get_function_configuration(FunctionName=function_name)` to access the `Configuration.Environment` variables.
- Check each environment variable's value for long encrypted values typical of KMS encrypted data, resembling a pattern such as `AQICAHhxbKJYcFAU16CbU4IVpzi5CwK`.
- The program should iterate through all available AWS regions since Lambda functions can exist in multiple regions.
- Ensure the compliance status by highlighting any environment variables that do not meet the encryption pattern.
- Example Pass Criteria: Environment variable values should show encryption, typically appearing as a long, formatted string.
- Example Fail Criteria: Environment variable values appearing as plain text.

This automated check efficiently utilizes AWS services’ capabilities to ensure that Lambda environment variables are encrypted in transit, aligning with the compliance requirements.
